zh.Great Coxwell, Vale of White Horse Distr: 英國英格蘭奧可福爾瓦爾 本 懷特 霍爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Morris於1523年出生在英國波克夏可維爾時，她的父親Sir Thomas Cordray Morris42歲，母親Alice Wynninge41歲。她於大約1534年與Thomas Cordray III在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1598年10月28日在英國英格蘭維爾爾特享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾特。

zh-hans.Great Coxwell, Vale of White Horse Distr: 英国英格兰奧可福爾瓦爾 本 懷特 霍爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Morris于1523年出生在英国波克夏可維爾时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Cordray Morris42岁，母亲Alice Wynninge41岁。她于大约1534年与Thomas Cordray III在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1598年10月28日在英国英格兰維爾爾特享年75岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾特。

nameMeanings.name: Morris
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from Maurice, an Old French personal name introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from Latin Mauritius, a derivative of Maurus (see Moore ). This was the name of several early Christian saints. In some cases it may be a nickname of the same derivation for someone with a swarthy complexion.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muirghis, a variant of Ó Muirgheasa (see Morrissey ).

Welsh: Anglicized form of the personal name Meurig (from Latin Mauritius), which was gradually superseded in Wales by Morus, Morys, a derivative of the Anglo-Norman French form of the name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Mauritz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Armin, Frieda, Helmut, Kurt, Reinhold, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from the personal name Mauritz, from Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Mauricio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Mario, Roberto, Lilia, Luis, Adela, Andres, Antero, Cipriano.  Portuguese Manoel.
Portuguese (Maurício) and Spanish: from the personal name Maurício (Portuguese), Mauricio (Spanish), derived from the Latin name Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Maurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Armand, Denys, Gabrielle, Antoine, Benoit, Camille, Cecile, Francois, Gaston, Germain.
French, English, Welsh, Scottish, and Irish: from the Old French personal name Maurice, Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: McMorris
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muiris, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of Morris . In some cases this is a Gaelicized form of Fitzmaurice .
Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muirgheasa ‘son of Muirgheas’, a Gaelic personal name meaning ‘sea valor’ or ‘sea choice’. Compare Morrissey .

variants.name: Morici
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Gasper, Aldo, Angelo, Annamarie, Elvio, Giuseppe, Sal, Santo, Vito.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a variant of the personal name Maurice (see Morris ).
Hungarian: patronymic from the personal name Móric, Hungarian form of Morris .

variants.name: Moris
meanings: English: variant of   Morris  .

French, Luxembourgish, Flemish, Walloon, North German, and Dutch: from a vernacular form of the French personal name Maurice (see Morris ). In North America, the surname Moris may also be an altered form of French Maurice .
Americanized form of German   Moritz  .

variants.name: Merrick
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Meuric, the Middle Welsh form of Maurice (see Morris ).
Irish: borrowed from Welsh. In Ireland, when this is not the Welsh name, it might be adopted for Irish Ó Mearadhaigh, see Merry 2.

variants.name: Maurizio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Marcello, Marco, Mario, Fernando, Matteo, Natale, Sal, Valentino, Vito.
Italian: from the personal name Maurizio, from Latin Mauritius (see Morris 1).

variants.name: Morehouse
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name from any of the many places called Moorhouse in Yorkshire or Durham (from Old English mōr ‘moor’ + hūs ‘house’). In some cases the surname may have become Morris . This name is also established in Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Morris was born in 1523, in Coxwell, Berkshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas Cordray Morris, was 42 and her mother, Alice Wynninge, was 41. She married Thomas Cordray III about 1534, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 28 October 1598, in Chute, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75, and was buried in Chute, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Thomas Cordray III environ 1534, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 octobre 1598, à Chute, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à Chute, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.Great Coxwell, Vale of White Horse Distr: 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 발 프 휘트 호르스
ko.lifeSummary: Jane Morris(이)가 1523년 영국 버크실르 콕스웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Cordray Morris(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Wynninge(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 대략 1534년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Thomas Cordray III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1598년 10월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 c후트에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 c후트에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Cordray III aproximadamente 1534, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 5 filhas. ru.Great Coxwell, Vale of White Horse Distr: веил оф уит хаурс, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Morris родилась в 1523 г. в местности коксвелл, биркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Cordray Morris было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Wynninge, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Cordray III около 1534 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 28 октября 1598 г. в местности чут, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности чут, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.
